All skills defer to orchestrator for routing

orchestrator. Invoke /production-grade to auto-route." This ensures

Implement task 50: temporal types (date/datetime/duration)

- Add PropertyValue::Date(i32), DateTime(i64), Duration{months,days,millis} to maharit-core

Add Tauri app, new crates, skills, PC control, studio chat, and dashboard updates

bundled skills system, studio chat sessions, provider key management,
convergence propagation, and updated .gitignore. Removes explore/ docs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Add Lighthouse CI and optimize web performance (#68)

* Optimize first paint time and add Lighthouse CI

- Defer /auth/check script to unblock HTML parsing (downloads in parallel,
  preserves execution order via defer)
- Make Font Awesome CSS non-render-blocking with media=print/onload pattern
- Move Google Fonts from SCSS @import to HTML <link> with display=swap to
  prevent Flash of Invisible Text
- Add preconnect hints for fonts.googleapis.com, fonts.gstatic.com, and
  cdnjs.cloudflare.com
- Add inline loading spinner for immediate first contentful paint
- Lazy-load HomeView/LandingView in IndexView via defineAsyncComponent
- Lazy-load SignupView, SignoutView, and 404View route components
- Add Lighthouse CI GitHub Action that posts scores on PRs
- Add .lighthouserc.json with performance/a11y/best-practices/SEO thresholds

https://claude.ai/code/session_015X77rJ4CmU1prQEuZ5XEJ5

* Reduce render-blocking resources and unused CSS/JS

Addresses Lighthouse critical issues:

- Make Google Fonts non-render-blocking (media=print/onload pattern,
  est savings ~810ms)
- Selective Bulma imports: skip unused modules (breadcrumb, dropdown, menu,
  message, modal, pagination, table) reducing app.css by 22 KiB
- Trim Sailwind utilities: reduce sizes from 24 to 7, remove unused gray
  colors/bg-*/maxw-* generators, trim weights to 2 and percents to 1
- Defer Zod to async chunk: change store imports from static to dynamic,
  moving Zod (16.7 KiB) out of the initial vendor bundle into a lazy chunk
  loaded only when validation actions are called
- Replace synchronous Zod validation of window.user_data with lightweight
  shape check; full Zod validation runs asynchronously in initialize()

Results: entrypoint reduced from 509 KiB to 424 KiB (-17%),
vendor JS -57 KiB, app CSS -22 KiB, app JS -4.5 KiB

https://claude.ai/code/session_015X77rJ4CmU1prQEuZ5XEJ5

* Migrate from Vue CLI (webpack) to Vite and fix Lighthouse CI

Build system migration:
- Replace Vue CLI 5 / webpack with Vite 7 for faster builds (25.9s -> 6.8s)
- Create vite.config.ts with Vue plugin, path aliases, SCSS config,
  and manual chunk splitting (vendor-vue, vendor-trpc)
- Move index.html from public/ to root (Vite convention), replace EJS
  templates with static HTML, add <script type="module"> entry point
- Replace process.env.BASE_URL with import.meta.env.BASE_URL in router
- Update tsconfig.json: webpack-env types -> vite/client types
- Remove babel.config.js, vue.config.js, and Vue CLI / Babel / sass-loader
  dependencies (Vite handles SCSS natively)
- Update package.json scripts: serve/build/lint now use Vite/ESLint directly
- Silence Bulma Sass deprecation warnings via silenceDeprecations config

Lighthouse CI fix:
- Inject mock auth data (dist/auth/check) before Lighthouse run so it
  tests the logged-in dashboard view instead of the 400 error page

https://claude.ai/code/session_015X77rJ4CmU1prQEuZ5XEJ5

* Fix Lighthouse CI: inline mock auth data in index.html

The previous approach of creating a dist/auth/check file didn't work
because the static server can't serve extensionless files with the
correct JavaScript Content-Type. Instead, use sed to replace the
<script defer src="/auth/check"> tag with an inline <script> containing
mock auth data directly in dist/index.html after build. This ensures
the app boots in logged-in mode and Lighthouse tests the dashboard view.

https://claude.ai/code/session_015X77rJ4CmU1prQEuZ5XEJ5

---------

Co-authored-by: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
DeFi Liquidation Cascade Predictor - TGN with temporal attention, feature gating, monotonicity loss

Single-cell Colab notebook with full 9-phase pipeline:
- TGN: wired TemporalAttentionLayer (was dead code), added feature gate,
  time-aware memory fusion, multi-scale temporal context, wider prediction heads
- Feature augmentation: 7 channels (current/mean/std/deviation/min/max/trend) over 30-day window
- MonotonicityRegularization: enforces P(longer horizon) >= P(shorter horizon)
- LSTM fix: added LayerNorm + gradient clipping + lower LR
- All source files, config, TVL data embedded in single executable cell

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: update color scale to red→orange→yellow→green→blue + apply scores to library discovery

- Color scale now: red (≤3) → orange (≤5) → yellow (≤7) → green (≤8.5) → blue (9+)
- Apply applyStoredScores to library Must Watch, mood picks, scenario results
  so ratings match the details page (Syllabus score instead of TMDB vote_average)
- Update tailwind safelist for new gradient classes

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

feat(gaps-1-4): email service, password reset, metric RBAC enforcement

Gap 1 — Invite emails now actually sent:
- services/email.py: async EmailService using stdlib smtplib + thread
  executor; falls back to console/log mode when SMTP_HOST is empty (dev)
- Settings: SMTP_HOST/PORT/USER/PASSWORD/FROM/TLS + RESET_TOKEN_TTL_MIN
- invite_user() calls email_svc.send_invite() after commit

Gap 2 — Password reset flow (end-to-end):
- models/password_reset_token.py: PasswordResetToken (user_id FK CASCADE,
  token_hash unique, expires_at, used_at)
- migration h7c8d9e0f1g2: creates password_reset_tokens table + indexes
- repositories/user.py: create/get/mark_used reset token + update_password
- schemas/user.py: ForgotPasswordRequest, ResetPasswordRequest (w/ strength)
- services/user.py: forgot_password (silent if unknown email — no leak),
  reset_password (validates token, updates pw, revokes all refresh tokens)
- routes/auth.py: POST /auth/forgot-password (rate-limited 5/min, always 204)
  and POST /auth/reset-password (204 on success)
- Frontend: ForgotPasswordPage (/forgot-password), ResetPasswordPage
  (/reset-password?token=...), "Forgot password?" link in LoginForm,
  success banner on login page after reset/invite accept

Gaps 3 & 4 — Metric write endpoints enforce analyst+ at route layer:
- create_metric_series: require_role(["admin","analyst"])
- update_metric_series: require_role(["admin","analyst"])
- upsert_metric_points: require_role(["admin","analyst"])
  (service-layer viewer checks retained for defence-in-depth)

Tests: 101 backend + 125 frontend — all pass.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
M11 — P4 arbre enrichi: hash, temperature, budgets dynamiques

- tree.json: hash SHA-256 (8 chars) par noeud pour detecter changements
- temperature auto-calculee: access_count + recency + fill ratio (0.0-1.0)
- budgets dynamiques par temperature (COCOM 2025): hot=1.3x, cold=0.6x
- prune R4 utilise temperature au lieu de access_count brut
- boot R7 utilise temperature pour ranking branches
- status affiche barre visuelle [======----] par noeud

Inspire de COCOM (Rau 2025): compression variable par importance.
Root t=0.68, branches actives t=0.53, froides t=0.30.

commit.message:
fix: 파일 삭제/리네임 시 항상 싱크 트리거

삭제와 리네임은 명확한 사용자 의도이므로 syncOnCreateDeleteRename
설정과 무관하게 항상 debounced sync를 트리거한다.
이전에는 설정이 false(기본값)일 때 삭제가 다음 interval/longpoll까지
반영되지 않아 conflict 파일이 다시 내려오는 문제가 있었다.

설정은 이제 파일 생성(create)에만 적용된다.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
fix: add sync timeout, faster stuck recovery, and self-chaining for queue

Three fixes for jobs getting stuck forever:

1. Sync timeout (22s) — wraps executeAdsSync/executeGTMSync in a
   timeout so they can't hang until Vercel kills the function. Timed-out
   jobs get retried automatically.

2. Faster stuck recovery — threshold lowered from 60s to 35s (Vercel
   hard-kills at 30s, so a PROCESSING job older than 35s is guaranteed dead).

3. Self-chaining — after processing loop ends, if QUEUED jobs remain,
   fires a fetch to /api/internal/process-queue which triggers another
   waitUntil(runProcessingLoop()). This continues until all jobs are done.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

refactor: remove BIG/SMALL CHANGE quota and improve issue description format (#4)

Three problems addressed in review-protocol.md:

1. Remove Phase 0 auto-detect and SMALL/BIG CHANGE distinction
   - Eliminated the per-area quota system that forced model to manufacture issues
   - Replaced with "Report Guidelines": report only real issues, up to 4 per area
   - Removed {REVIEW_MODE} placeholder from prompt templates and report format

2. Expand Problem field to What/Why/Impact structure
   - Old: single-line "Concrete description of the issue"
   - New: What (what is wrong) / Why (root cause) / Impact (actual harm or risk)
   - Applied to both Plan review and Code review prompt templates

3. Tone down Engineering Standards to reduce over-engineering pressure
   - DRY: "aggressively" → "only when it causes real maintenance problems"
   - Well-tested: "rather have too many" → "focus on critical paths and edge cases"
   - Added explicit principle: "no issue is a valid finding"

Applied to: Engineering Preferences block, Review Prompt Template, Code Review Prompt Template

Co-authored-by: sanghyun-qesg <shkim@qesg.co.kr>

commit.message:
Initial GawdBot — self-evolving AI assistant

Combines thepopebot (self-evolution via git), claude-telegram-relay
(message queue, memory, voice transcription), and ada_local (wake word,
STT/TTS pipeline) into a unified Python project.

Features:
- NVIDIA NIM + Ollama backends (OpenAI-compatible, one env var to switch)
- Telegram bot with message queue, rate limiting, and voice message support
- Wake word (openwakeword) + faster-whisper STT + Piper TTS
- SQLite persistent memory with embedding-based semantic search
- Coding agent: file I/O, shell commands, git operations, GitHub PRs
- PC control: mouse, keyboard, windows, clipboard, screenshots (pyautogui)
- Webhook server: POST /webhook to trigger agent, POST /webhook/embed for embeddings
- Self-evolution: /evolve command creates a branch, edits own code, opens PR

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(vdg): serialization seam — semantic_score + marengo_embedding survive VDGv4 pipeline

VDGUnifiedResult→VDGv4 conversion was silently dropping semantic_score
and marengo_embedding, causing DB writes of 0/NULL despite correct
pipeline computation. Root cause: fields missing from Pydantic schema
and converter pass-through.

- Add semantic_score/marengo_embedding to VDGv4 schema
- Pass both fields through convert_unified_to_vdg_v4()
- Switch analyzer quality gate to validate_full() with semantic_score
- Add semantic_score to CANONICAL_FIELDS for read-path normalization
- Fix false-confidence test to verify converter→model_dump() path
- Add 5 seam regression tests (19/19 passing)

commit.message:
Support both Spotify data format options (standard + extended)

Enables users to upload immediately with standard Account Data
(StreamingHistory_music_*.json) while also supporting Extended
Streaming History for full geographic features.

WHAT CHANGED:
- Format detection: identify standard vs extended format per entry
- Normalization: convert standard to extended schema for parser
- Cross-format dedup: if both formats uploaded, extend data wins
- Validation: accept both 'ms_played' and 'msPlayed' field names

FEATURES NOW SUPPORTED:
Standard format (immediate download):
  ✅ Timeline, Eras, Year Review, Life Staples, Taste Passport
  ✅ Activity Calendar
  ⚠️ Map shows US only (no country data in standard format)

Extended format (5-30 days):
  ✅ Everything above
  ✅ Full map with real per-country listening data

Mixed upload (both formats):
  ✅ Automatically deduplicates overlapping plays
  ✅ Extended data takes priority

UPDATED UI:
- Upload hint: lists both file name patterns
- Info panel: two-path instructions explaining trade-offs
- Clear note that both formats can be uploaded together

IMPLEMENTATION:
- Added isBasicEntry() and normalizeBasicEntry() helpers
- Updated validation to check for both camelCase and snake_case
- Added dedup logic using synthetic URI for basic entries
- Replaced single-path instructions with comparative two-option layout

fix(db): restore orphaned migration and fix ordering for production deploys (#82)

PR #47 replaced the journal entry for 0003_rename-user-roles with
0003_add-artist-image-path at the same timestamp. On production DBs where
the rename migration was already applied, Drizzle's timestamp-based
migrator silently skipped the image_path migration, causing scan failures
with "column image_path of relation artists does not exist".

Restores the rename migration to the journal and moves the image_path
migration to idx 5 with a higher timestamp so it runs on all existing
databases. Both migrations are now idempotent.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
